etymology.description: phonetic 化(化) (huà, "to transform") + semantic 十(十) (shí, "stalk and leaves") -> 華(華) (huá, "magnificent")
etymology.interpretation: 華(華) (huá, "magnificent") combines 化(化) (huà, "to transform") providing the pronunciation, with 十(十) (shí, "stalk and leaves") symbolizing growth and abundance, resulting in the sense of magnificence and richness. This imagery naturally bridges to the idea of 'magnificent', depicting flourishing plants as a symbol of splendor, which extends to refer to China as a culturally rich and illustrious place.
etymology.simplification: 华(華): The top component of 華(華) was simplified by using 化(化) (huà, "to transform") in the simplified version 华, retaining phonetic resemblance while removing the detailed component 十(十).

examples.chinese: 中国是一个充满魅力的国家(中國是一個充滿魅力的國家)。
examples.pinyin: Zhōngguó shì yīge chōngmǎn mèilì de guójiā.
examples.english: China is a country full of charm.

examples.chinese: 他的家在华北平原(他的家在華北平原)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de jiā zài Huáběi píngyuán.
examples.english: His home is on the North China Plain.

examples.chinese: 这座建筑非常华丽(這座建築非常華麗)。
